title: Apple’s China App Store Fee Cut Tests Services Margins And Regulation
summary: Apple has reduced its standard App Store commission in mainland China from 30% to 25% following regulatory pressure. Certain fees will be cut further to 12% for small business and mini app developers, reshaping the economics for many app makers. The move affects Apple in its second largest market and could influence how regulators and platforms approach app store fees elsewhere. For investors watching NasdaqGS:AAPL, this update relates to one of Apple's key service lines rather than its...

title: 1 Consumer Stock with Solid Fundamentals and 2 We Turn Down
summary: Consumer discretionary businesses are levered to the highs and lows of economic cycles. This sensitive demand profile can cause discretionary stocks to plummet when macro uncertainty enters the fray, and over the past six months, the industry has shed 9.8%. This drawdown is a far cry from the S&P 500’s 2.3% ascent.
